revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Hinomisaki Shrine]][[de:Hinomisaki-Schrein]][[en:Hinomisaki Shrine]][[ja:日御碕神社]][[simple:Hinomisaki Shrine]][[zh:日御碕神社]] {{Expand Japanese|日御碕神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Hinomisaki Shrine | native_name = 日御碕神社 | image = Hinomisaki-jinja Two-storied Gate 001.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hinomisaki Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.42958056|132.62936111}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu|ar|أماتيراسو|ary|أماطيراسو|az|Amaterasu|bcl|Amaterasu|be_x_old|Аматэрасу|be|Аматэрасу|bew|Amatérasu|bg|Аматерасу|ca|Amaterasu|cs|Amaterasu|da|Amaterasu|de|Amaterasu|el|Αματεράσου|en|Amaterasu|eo|Amaterasu|es|Amaterasu|et|Amaterasu|eu|Amaterasu|fa|آماتراسو|fi|Amaterasu|fr|Amaterasu|gl|Amaterasu|he|אמטראסו|hi|अमातेरसु|hr|Amaterasu|hu|Amateraszu Ómikami|hy|Ամատերասու Օմիկամի|id|Amaterasu|it|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|jaquote|アマテラス|jv|Amaterasu|ka|ამატერასუ|ko|아마테라스 오미카미|la|Amaterasu|lb|Amaterasu|lt|Amaterasu|lv|Amaterasu|min|Amaterasu|ml|അമതേരസു|mni|ꯑꯃꯇꯦꯔꯁꯨ ꯑꯣꯃꯤꯀꯃꯤ|ms|Amaterasu|nl|Amaterasu|nn|Amaterasu|no|Amaterasu|or|ଆମାତେରାଶୁ|pa|ਆਮਾਤੇਰਸ|pl|Amaterasu|pt|Amaterasu|ro|Amaterasu|ru|Аматэрасу|sco|Amaterasu|sh|Amaterasu|simple|Amaterasu Ōmikami|sk|Amaterasu Ómikami|sl|Amaterasu|sr|Аматерасу|su|Amaterasu|sv|Amaterasu|ta|அமதெரசு|th|อามาเตราซุ|tl|Amaterasu|tr|Amaterasu|uk|Аматерасу|uz|Amaterasu|vi|Amaterasu|wuu|天照大神|zh_min_nan|Amaterasu|zh_yue|天照大神|zh|天照大神|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} }} {{nihongo|'''Hinomisaki Shrine'''|日御碕神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần Province|lt=Izumo Province|ar|إزومو (مقاطعة)|ca|Província d'Izumo|cs|Provincie Izumo|de|Provinz Izumo|en|Izumo Province|es|Provincia de Izumo|fa|ولایت ایزومو|fr|Province d'Izumo|gl|Provincia de Torii on street.jpg == museum-digital place ID (P9957) == * 32534 {{commons category|Category:Hinomisaki Shrine}} [[DE:Hinomisaki-Schrein]] [[EN:Hinomisaki Shrine]] [[JA:日御碕神社]] [[SIMPLE:Hinomisaki Shrine]] [[ZH:日御碕神社]] {{wikidata link|Q1619446}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name = Hinomisaki Shrine | Image=[[Image:Hinomisaki-jinja Two-storied Gate 001.jpg|270px]]
Romon | Address={{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|qid=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|qid=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}}455 Hinomisaki, Taisha-cho | Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Sujinson|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi-sha|1=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system #government shrine|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Kokuhei-kosha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}
{{ill|lt=Beppyo Shrine|Q10898274|qid=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} | Founded={{ill|lt=Emperor An'ei|Q313345|qid=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}}13 | Main shrine={{ill|lt=Gongenzo|Q3155268|qid=Q3155268|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} | Temples, etc.={{ill|lt=Izumo Kuni Shinto and Buddhist Sacred Sites|Q11395860|qid=Q11395860|1=Izumo Shinbutsu Pilgrimage}}No. 20 | Annual festival={{ill|lt=August 7th|Q2781|qid=Q2781|1=August 7}} | Shinto rituals = Oyori Shinto ritual, Goban Shinto ritual, Toshishi festival, Wasukari Shinto ritual, Reitaisai festival/Shinko festival, Tsumebari festival, Jinzai festival/Kamiryade festival, Shinkuruboten Shinto ritual | Map={{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Hinomisaki Shrine|lat_deg=35|lat_min=25|lat_sec=46.4|lon_deg=132|lon_min=37|lon_sec=45.5|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point |zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|25|46.4|N|132|37|45.5|E}} }} |Alternative name=Commonly known as “Misaki-san”|Location={{coord|35|25|46.4|N|132|37|45.5|E|region:JP-32_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} [[File:Hinomisaki-jinja Worship Hall of Hishizuminomiya (Shrine) 001.jpg|thumb|Hinomisaki-jinja Worship Hall of Hishizuminomiya (Shrine)
Hinomisaki-jinja Worship Hall (Important Cultural Property)]][[File:Hinomisaki-jinja Worship Hall of kaminomiya (Shrine) 001.jpg|thumb|Kaminomiya (Kaminomiya)
Worship Hall (Important Cultural Property)]] '''Hinomisaki Shrine'' (Hinomisaki Shrine) is located in {{ill|lt=Hinosaki|Q11505328|qid=Q11505328|1=Cape Hinomisaki}} in {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|qid=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Izumo City|Q825166|qid=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}}. Commonly known as ``Misaki-san.'' He is revered as the ``Oyagami-sama'' of {{ill|lt=Izumo Taisha|Q696362|qid=Q696362|1=Izumo-taisha}}. Twelve shrine buildings and other buildings have been designated as national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Properties|Q1188622|qid=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. ===History=== Our company has two shrines, upper and lower. The upper shrine is called ``Kami no Miya,'' and the lower shrine is called ``Hishizuminomiya,'' and the two shrines are collectively called ``Hinosaki Shrine'' (''Hinosaki Daijingu Shrine''). In ancient times, Misaki Shrine is mentioned in ``{{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|qid=Q11395858|1=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki}}'' and ``Misaki Shrine'' is mentioned in ``{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}{{Sfn|Shirai|1997|p=290}}, and the locals call it ``Misaki-san.''{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.izumo.shimane.jp/www/contents/1260146321415/files/p16-17.pdf |title=Public Relations Izumo No. 114 |access-date=2022-07-27 |publisher=Izumo City |format=PDF}}.According to the shrine's legend, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Suenarison|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, after the nation-building of {{ill|lt=Izumo|Q11395836|qid=Q11395836|1=Izumo}}, became Kumanariminecurrently Mt. Tengu. It is located on the border between Yakumo Village, Yatsuka District, Shimane Prefecture, and Hirose Town, Nogi District, and was once called Mt. Kumanu.In the Izumo Fudoki, it is described as ``Kumano Mountain.The so-called shrine seat of Kumano Okami.''Near the top of the mountain, there is a huge rock where Kumano Okami was enshrined in the former shrine of {{ill|lt=Kumano Taisha|Q11568887|qid=Q11568887|1=Kumano Taisha}}. , looking for a place to rest, he read the oak leaves with the wind and came to a stop on the hill behind the Himisaki Shrine/Kamimiya Shrine. It is said that his son, {{ill|lt=Ama no Fuyui no Kami|Q48744862|qid=Q48744862|lt=Amanzukine no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, enshrined his soul there, and Kakuregaoka ({{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|qid=Q1141225|1=Kofun}}) is currently located behind the main shrine building.{{Sfn|Shirai|1997|p=290}}. Sunset Shrine was originally enshrined in Fukushima (currently {{ill|lt=Keijima|Q5508550|qid=Q5508550|1=Fumi-shima}}), and when Amazukine no Mikoto was in Fukushima, {{jalink|Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602} } is said to have descended and was enshrined with a divine edict that said, ``I will bless the people of the world, so please worship me quickly.''{{Sfn|Shirai|1997|p=290}} is a holy place for ascetic training and is described as a ``holy address'' in ``{{ill|lt=Ryojin Hisho|Q3010584|qid=Q3010584|1=Ryōjin Hishō}}'' compiled by {{ill|lt=Emperor Goshirakawa|Q317984|qid=Q317984|lt=Retired Emperor Goshirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa}} at the end of the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. The Shrine of the Gods (Kami no Miya) was currently enshrined on Kakuregaoka Hill behind the main shrine, but in the 13th year of Emperor An'ei ({{ill|lt=6th century BC|Q25296|qid=Q25296|lt=536 BC|1=6th century BC}}), it was moved to its current location by imperial order, and Nishingu Shrine (Lower Palace) It is said that it was enshrined on {{ill|lt=Keijima|Q5508550|qid=Q5508550|lt=Keijima (Hikijima)|1=Fumi-shima}}, but was moved to its current location in {{ill|lt=Tenryaku|Q1346281|qid=Q1346281|1=Tenryaku}}2 (948) by imperial order of Emperor Murakami.. Since ancient times, it has been highly revered by the {{ill|lt=Imperial Court (Japan)|Q30253177|qid=Q30253177|lt=Imperial Court|1=Imperial Court of Japan}}, and since the {{ill|lt=Kamakura Period|Q236205|qid=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, it has been revered by the {{ill|lt=Bakufu|Q1074150|qid=Q1074150|1=Shogunate}}, and the shrine has been renovated. {{ill|lt=Izumo Province|Q582846|qid=Q582846|1=Izumo Province}}{{ill|lt=Matsue Domain|Q1017072|qid=Q1017072|1=Matsue Domain}}'s first generation, {{ill|lt=Tadauji Horio|Q3140540|qid=Q3140540|1=Horio Tadauji}}, gave a shrine territory of over 780 koku, and the Tokugawa shogunate also gave 600 koku {{Sfn|Shirai|1997|p=290}}. It is said to be the successor shrine to Izumo Taisha in the Sanin region {{Sfn|Great Dictionary of Shinto|1969|p=200}}.The current shrine building of Nissu Shrine, God's Palace, was built by the {{ill|lt=Edo Shogunate|Q205662|qid=Q205662|1=Tokugawa shogunate}}3rd generation {{ill|lt=shogun|Q15903946|qid=Q15903946|1=jiangjun}} and {{jalink|virtue}. By order of Kawaiemitsu|Q314481}}, {{ill|lt=Matsue Domain|Q1017072|qid=Q1017072|1=Matsue Domain}} lord {{ill|lt=Kyogoku Tadaka|Q11374423|qid=Q11374423|1=Kyōgoku Tadataka}}, {{j alink|Nikko Toshogu|Q696641}} Immediately after the completion of {{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|qid=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}}11 ({{jalink|1634|Q6872} }) Construction began in the 21st year of the Kan'ei era ({{ill|lt=1644|Q6920|qid=Q6920|1=1644}}) and was completed during the reign of {{ill|lt=Naomasa Matsudaira|Q5324802|qid=Q5324802|1=Matsudaira Naomasa}}. Since then, it has been revered by successive feudal lords {{Sfn|Great Dictionary of Shinto|1969|p=200}}. Both shrines are in Gongen style modeled after Nikko Toshogu Shrine. The sculptures of three monkeys and a dragon are also identical to those of Nikko Toshogu Shrine. {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}In 4, he was ranked as Kokuheishosha. The name ``Sunset Shrine'' originates from the fact that, while {{ill|lt=Ise Jingu|Q687168|qid=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} was built to ``protect the day of the book of the day,'' Hinomisaki Shrine received an ``imperial command'' to ``protect the night of the book of the day.''It was installed locally, according to the history board of Hinosaki Shrine. . Twelve shrine buildings, including Nissu Shrine, Shrine of the Gods, Romon, Corridor, Purification Place, Treasure House, and Monkyakujinsha, as well as two {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|qid=Q234731|1=Torii}} and five stone {{ill|lt=lanterns|Q12012294|qid=Q12012294|lt=lantern|1=Stone lantern}}, have been designated as important cultural properties of the country. ===Enshrined God=== The following deities are enshrined {{Sfn|Great Dictionary of Shinto|1969|p=200}}. ; Sunset Palace (Lower Palace) :; Main enshrined deity ::* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=UNKNOWN}} :; Deity enshrined ::* {{ill|lt=Amenooshihomimi|Q462555|qid=Q462555|lt=Tenninhomison|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}} ::* {{ill|lt=Amenohohi|Q10940685|qid=Q10940685|lt=Amaho Nichimiko|1=Ame no Hohi}} ::* {{ill|lt=Amatsuhikone|Q10940228|qid=Q10940228|lt=Amatsu Hikone Mikoto|1=Amatsuhikone}} ::* {{ill|lt=Ikutsuhikone|Q11147583|qid=Q11147583|lt=Katsu Hikone Mikoto|1=Ikutsuhikone}} ::* {{ill|lt=Kumano Kusubi|Q11298796|qid=Q11298796|lt=Kumano Shojimikoto|1=Kumanokusubi}} ; God's Temple (Upper Temple) :; Main enshrined deity ::* {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} :; Deity enshrined ::* {{ill|lt=Takiribime|Q10932870|qid=Q10932870|lt=Tashinhime no Mikoto|1=Tagorihime}} ::* {{ill|lt=Tagitsuhime|Q10932592|qid=Q10932592|lt=Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|1=Tagitsuhime}} ::* {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|qid=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime|1=Ichikishima-hime}} === Precincts === [[File:Hinomisaki-jinja and Fumi Island Aerial photograph1976.jpg|thumb|Aerial photograph of Hinomisaki-jinja Shrine and the area around {{ill|lt=Keijima|Q5508550|qid=Q5508550|1=Fumi-shima}} (former and enshrining place of Hinomisaki Shrine and Shimonomiya)
Taken in 1976
{{National aerial photograph}}. ]] *Hereafter (Important Monuments): Important cultural properties of the country. * Torii: (important cultural property) :: There is a {{ill|lt=granite|Q41177|qid=Q41177|lt=granite|1=Granite}} built torii gate in the position where the tower gate can be seen, but it was originally built at the entrance of the approach to Uryu (Izumo City {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Taisha Town|1=UNKNOWN}}), but was moved to its current location in {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|qid=Q18658|1=1935}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}10). An inscription was found during the move, and it was determined that it was donated by Iemitsu Tokugawa in 1639 ({{ill|lt=1639|Q6897|qid=Q6897|1=1639}}). The torii gate at the entrance to Kiyoe Beach on the west side of the shrine was also donated by Iemitsu. |publisher=Izumo City Citizens' Culture Department, Cultural Properties Division}}. * Chozuya * Shrine office * Romon: (important cultural property) * Corridor: (Important Monument) * Stone lantern: (important cultural property) * Misogi place: (Important cultural property) * Treasure trove: (important cultural property) * Shinmon Sekisha File:Hinomisaki Shrine Torii on street.jpg|alt=|Torii (relocated from Uryu) File:Hinomisaki-jinja kairo2.jpg|Shimonomiya Corridor File:Hinomisaki-jinja misogisho.jpg|Shimonomiya Misogisho File:Hinomisaki-jinja treasure house 001.jpg|alt=|treasure house * Sunset Palace (Lower Palace): (Important Cultural Property) *: It is said that ``Hyakki Shrine'', which had been enshrined on Keijima (Hikijima) in front of Kiyoe Beach, just outside the shrine grounds, was moved to its current location in 948 by an order of Emperor Murakami. The interior is divided into an upper and a lower tier, and according to ancient documents, the upper tier is said to have been called the ``Kagura-sho'' . ** Main shrine ** Seiden ** Worship Hall ** Tamagaki * Temple of God (Upper Shrine): (Important Monument) *: It was originally enshrined on the hill behind the current shrine building, but it is said to have been moved to its current location in the 13th year of Emperor An'ei. ** Main shrine ** Seiden ** Worship Hall ** Tamagaki File:Hinomisaki-jinja Hinomisaki Shrine Main Sanctuary of Hishizuminomiya (Shrine) 001.jpg|alt=|Nishingu Shrine (Lower Shrine)
Oratory (left), main shrine (back right), Tamagaki (surrounding the main shrine)File:Hinomisaki-jinja Hinomisaki Shrine Main Sanctuary of kaminomiya (Shrine) 001.jpg|alt=|Kaminomiya (upper shrine)
Main shrine (left), Tamagaki (surrounding the main shrine), Worship hall (right) === Setsusha === [[File:Hinomisaki-jinja Fumi-Shima-jinja in Fumi Island 002.jpg|thumb|Kyojima Shrine
The former enshrinement site of Nishingu Shrine (Shimonomiya)]] * Kadomarodosha: (Important Cultural Property) *: Immediately after entering the tower gate, it is located on the left side (south side) of the Sunshingu Shrine (Shimonomiya) shrine. ** Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Tenshimonbetsukami|Q10940542|qid=Q10940542|lt=Toyowamatokami|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} * Kadomarodosha: (Important Cultural Property) *: Immediately after entering the tower gate, it is enshrined on the right side (north side) of Nishingu Shrine (Shimonomiya) shrine. ** Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Tenshimonbetsukami|Q10940542|qid=Q10940542|lt=Kushiwamatokami|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} * Ebijisha * Jukusha Setsusha
:* Hiywasaki Shrine :* Hidai Shrine :* Sono Wakahime Shrine :* Oyamazumi Shrine :* Ibomi Shrine * Hachi Shrine * Oto Shrine * Tachibana Shrine * Nakatsu Shrine * Uga Shrine * Cave Shrine * Mano Shrine * Ono Shrine * Ma Shrine * Kaga Shrine * Sakado Shrine * Wakamiya * Otoshi Shrine * Hachiman Shrine
* Aratama Shrine * Ara Matsuri Shrine * Mii Shrine * Inari Shrine * Korean Shrine * Munakata Shrine``Kingaisha'''' * Tsunejima Shrine (Motomiya) *: Located on Keijima (Hioki Island), where Nishingu Shrine (Shimonomiya) was originally enshrined, and where there is currently a small shrine. ** Enshrined deity: Amazukine no Mikoto File:Hinomisaki-jinja monkakujinshauden.jpg|alt=Shinonomiya Monkyakujinsha (south) | Shimonomiya Monkyakujinsha (south)
Sunset Shrine (Shimonomiya) Left side facing the shrine building File:Hinomisaki-jinja monkakujinshasaden.jpg|alt=Shinonomiya Monkyakujinsha (north)|Shinonomiya Monkyakujinsha (north)
Isshingu Shrine (Shimonomiya) Right side facing the shrine building File:Hinomisaki-jinja Auxiliary Hiruko-sya 001.jpg|alt=|Hirujisha File:Hinomisaki-jinja 19 Auxiliary Shrines 001.jpg|alt=|Jukusha Setsusha File:Hinomisaki-jinja Auxiliary Aratama-jinja.jpg|alt=|Aratama Shrine File:Hinomisaki-jinja Auxiliary Aramatsuri-no-miya 001.jpg|alt=|Aramatsuri Shrine File:Hinomisaki-jinja Auxiliary Mii-jinja 001.jpg|alt=|Mii Shrine File:Hinomisaki-jinja Auxiliary Inari-jinja 002.jpg|alt=|Inari Shrine ===Cultural assets=== === National Treasure === [[File:До-мару.JPG|thumb|Lan Weiwei belly band (exhibited at {{ill|lt=Tokyo National Museum|Q653433|qid=Q653433|1=Tokyo National Museum}})]] ; crafts :* White armor with helmet and large sleeves 1 territory - Designated March 31, 1953{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/401 |title=White armor (with helmet and large sleeves) / National treasure/Important cultural property (work of art) |access-date=2022-07-27 |publisher=Nationally designated cultural property database}}.::: Made in the Kamakura period. Body height: 63.6{{ill|lt=cm|Q174728|qid=Q174728|1=Centimetre}}, helmet height: 11.2cm, sleeve height: 39.4cm. During the Edo period, it was known as {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|qid=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} votive armor. At the end of the Edo period, the temple suffered severe damage such as its doshiito, and in 1805, by order of Harusato Matsudaira, the lord of the Matsue domain, it was repaired in Edo to its current form without damaging its original appearance. At that time, Shirokawa dyed with the words ``Bunka Ninen Repair'' was used to repair the damaged parts, and the remaining items such as the threads and cords that were removed are kept. Ataka Teramoto, who was in charge of the repairs, has written a 61-point repair record, ``Annotations for repairing the armor of Lord Minamoto no Yoritomo.'' |publisher=Tokyo National Museum}}. {{ill|lt=Tokyo National Museum|Q653433|qid=Q653433|1=Tokyo National Museum}} deposited ({{ill|lt=Takasada Shioji|Q11428604|qid=Q11428604|1=En'ya Takasada}} donation). === Important cultural property === ; building :* Hinomisaki Shrine 12 buildings, 2 statues - Designated on March 31, 1953. ::: Built in 1644. Built by order of Tokugawa Iemitsu. ::* Main shrine of Nishingu Shrine (Shinonomiya) 1 building{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2972 |title=Main shrine of Nishingu Shrine (Shinonomiya) / Hinomisaki Shrine / National treasure/Important cultural property (building) |access-date=2022-07-27 |publisher=Nationally designated cultural properties database / Agency for Cultural Affairs}} ::* Nisshingu Shrine (Shimonomiya) Heiden and Hall of Worship 1 building{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2973 |title=Nishingu Shrine (Shimonomiya) Heiden and Worship Hall / Hinomisaki Shrine / National Treasure and Important Cultural Property (Building) |access-date=2022-07-27 |publisher=Nationally designated cultural properties database / Agency for Cultural Affairs}}::* Sunshingu Shrine (Shinonomiya) Tamagaki Building 1{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2974 |title=Sunshingu Shrine (Shinonomiya) Tamagaki / Hinomisaki Shrine / National Treasure/Important Cultural Property (Building) |access-date=2022-07-27 |publisher=Nationally designated cultural properties database / Agency for Cultural Affairs}} ::* Sunshingu Shrine (Shinonomiya) Misogisho 1 building{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2975 |title=Sunshingu Shrine (Shinonomiya) Misogisho / Hinomisaki Shrine / National Treasure/Important Cultural Property (Building) |access-date=2022-07-27 |publisher=Nationally designated cultural properties database / Agency for Cultural Affairs}} ::* Sunshingu Shrine (Shimonomiya) Corridor 1 building{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2976 |title=Sunshingu Shrine (Shimonomiya) Corridor / Hinomisaki Shrine / National Treasure/Important Cultural Property (Building) |access-date=2022-07-27 |publisher=Nationally designated cultural properties database / Agency for Cultural Affairs}}::* Sunshingu Shrine (Shinonomiya) Tower Gate 1{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2977 |title=Sunshingu Shrine (Shinonomiya) Tower Gate / Hinomisaki Shrine / National Treasure/Important Cultural Property (Building) |access-date=2022-07-27 |publisher=Nationally designated cultural properties database / Agency for Cultural Affairs}} ::* Nishingu Shrine (Shinonomiya) Monkijinsha 2 buildings{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2978 |title=Nishingu Shrine (Shinonomiya) Monkijinsha (1) / Hinomisaki Shrine / National Treasure/Important Cultural Property (Building) |access-date=2022-07-27 |publisher=Database of nationally designated cultural properties / Agency for Cultural Affairs}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2979 |title=Hishingu Shrine (Shimonomiya) Monkyakujinsha (2) / Hinomisaki Shrine / National Treasures/Important Cultural Properties (Buildings) |access-date=2022-07-27 |publisher=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database / Agency for Cultural Affairs}}::* Main shrine of God's shrine (Kaminomiya) 1 building{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2980 |title=Main shrine of God's shrine (Kaminomiya) / Hinomisaki Shrine / National treasure/Important cultural property (building) |access-date=2022-07-27 |publisher=Nationally designated cultural properties database / Agency for Cultural Affairs}} ::* Shrine of the Gods (Kaminomiya) Heiden and worship hall 1 building{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2981 |title=Kami no Miya (Kaminomiya) Heiden and worship hall / Hinomisaki Shrine / National Treasure/Important Cultural Property (Building) |access-date=2022-07-27 |publisher=Nationally designated cultural properties database / Agency for Cultural Affairs}} ::* Kaminomiya (Kaminomiya) Tamagaki Building 1{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2982 |title=Kamimiya (Kaminomiya) Tamagaki / Hinomisaki Shrine / National Treasure/Important Cultural Property (Building) |access-date=2022-07-27 |publisher=Nationally designated cultural properties database / Agency for Cultural Affairs}}::* Shrine of the Gods (Kaminomiya) Treasure House 1 building{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2983 |title=Treasury of the Shrine of the Gods (Kaminomiya) / Hinomisaki Shrine / National Treasure/Important Cultural Property (Building) |access-date=2022-07-27 |publisher=Nationally designated cultural properties database / Agency for Cultural Affairs}} ::* 2 torii gates of the Shrine of the Gods (Kaminomiya) {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2984 |title=Torii of the Shrine of the Gods (Kaminomiya) / Hinomisaki Shrine / National Treasure/Important Cultural Property (Building) |access-date=2022-07-27 |publisher=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database / Agency for Cultural Affairs}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/2985 |title=Kaminomiya (Kaminomiya) Torii / Hinomisaki Shrine / National Treasure/Important Cultural Property (Building) |access-date=2022-07-27 |publisher=Nationally designated cultural properties database / Agency for Cultural Affairs}} :::: Below are the designated properties . :::* 1 volume of Hinomisaki Gotentate painted painted hardware :::* Izumo Province Hinomisaki Gozoei Ginko Uketori Douyo Bankbook 1 book :::* Hinomisaki shrine building plot map 19 sheets:::* Diagram of Hinomisaki Shrine, Volume 1 :::* 5 stone lanterns ; crafts :* Aikawa Odoshi Haramaki - Designated on March 31, 1953{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5550 |title=Aikawa Odoshi Haramaki / National Treasure/Important Cultural Property (Artwork) |access-date=2022-07-27 |publisher=Nationally designated cultural properties database / Agency for Cultural Affairs}}. ::: {{ill|lt=Nanbokucho period (Japan)|Q264012|qid=Q264012|lt=Nanbokucho period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}. {{ill|lt=Tokyo National Museum|Q653433|qid=Q653433|1=Tokyo National Museum}}Deposited. ({{ill|lt=Nawa Nagano|Q11415325|qid=Q11415325|1=Nawa Nagatoshi}} donation) *A "belly band" is a type of armor. === National Natural Monument === [[File:Hinomisaki-jinja Fumi-Shima-jinja in Fumi Island 001.jpg|thumb|Kyojima. There is Tsunejima Shrine, a shrine outside the precincts, and the former enshrinement site of Nishingu Shrine (Shimonomiya)]] * {{ill|lt=Keijima|Q5508550|qid=Q5508550|1=Fumi-shima}} Black-tailed black-tailed gull breeding ground - Designated on March 8, 1922 (Taisho 11){{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2151 |title=Keijima black-tailed black-tailed black-tailed breeding ground / Historic site, scenic beauty, and natural monument |access-date=2022-07-27 |publisher=Nationally designated cultural properties database / Agency for Cultural Affairs}}. :: Tsunejima Island owned by Hinomisaki Shrine and the former resting place of Sunset Shrine (Shinonomiya) (managed by Izumo City). {{ill|lt=Sea of ​​Japan|Q27092|qid=Q27092|1=Sea of Japan}}A typical {{ill|lt=gull-tailed-gull|Q623194|qid=Q623194|1=Black-tailed gull}} breeding ground in the western part of the country. === Prefectural designated cultural property === ; crafts :* Gun (by Kiyotaka) Attachment: Gun case and related documents - {{ill|lt=1962|Q2764|qid=Q2764|1=1962}} (designated June 12, 1962){{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/kougeihin.html |title=List of prefecture-designated cultural properties [Crafts] |access-date=2022-07-27 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Education Agency Cultural Properties Division}}. ::: Built in {{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}}17 ({{ill|lt=1612|Q6771|qid=Q6771|1=1612}}) :* 1 Shijuhachimensuji helmet with white shoulders and 1 pigeon tail plate - Designated on May 23, 1969 . ::: Early Muromachi period :* 熏wei wei throat ring 1 kake - Designated on May 23, 1969 (Showa 44). ::: Nanbokucho period :* Shiraito Takeshita Red Throat 1 Kake - Designated on May 23, 1969 (Showa 44) . ::: Late Muromachi period :* Large and small swords made by Yasutsugu Echizen, 1 set of 2 nashiji large and small swords, and 1 set of Koshirae. Included with: ``Izumo Kokuhinomi Sakita Shrine Seiden Transfer Procedures'' Volume 1 - Designated April 26, 1996 (Heisei 8) . ; calligraphy:* Paperback and ink ink, Kounmei, Wei, Nikosaki Shrine Construction Kanjinki, Volume 1 - Designated on May 23, 1969 (Showa 44) |access-date=2022-07-27 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Education Agency Cultural Properties Division}}. ::: {{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193078|qid=Q1193078|1=Ōei}}27 ({{ill|lt=1420|Q6209|qid=Q6209|1=1420}}) :* Paper and ink calligraphy of Hinomisaki Shrine kanka register, 2 volumes - Designated on August 12, 1975 (Showa 50) . ::: Made around 1524 ; canon :* Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki (Hinomisaki Shrine book) 1 volume - Designated on June 13, 1961 {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/tennseki.html |title=List of prefecture-designated cultural properties [canon] |access-date=2022-07-27 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Education Agency Cultural Properties Division}}. ::: Kanei 11 (1634) {{ill|lt=Kishu Tokugawa family|Q11605046|qid=Q11605046|lt=Kishu family|1=Kishū Tokugawa family}} Donation ; Important art object:* Sword inscription: Dedicated Izumo Province Hinomisaki Reishin Shigeyoshi Ono 1 mouth - Designated February 13, 1940 (Showa 15) |access-date=2022-07-27 |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Education Agency Cultural Properties Division}}. ::: Early Edo period ===Events=== * {{ill|lt=January 3rd|Q2156|qid=Q2156|1=January 3}} Miyori Shinto ritual * {{ill|lt=January 7th|Q2228|qid=Q2228|1=January 7}} Ceremony * {{ill|lt=January 11th|Q2247|qid=Q2247|1=January 11}} Eishi Festival * {{ill|lt=January 5th|Q2202|qid=Q2202|1=January 5}} (lunar calendar) Wabukari Shinto ritual * {{ill|lt=August 7th|Q2781|qid=Q2781|1=August 7}} Reitaisai/Shinkosai (Sunset Festival) * {{ill|lt=September 1st|Q2859|qid=Q2859|1=September 1}} Tsumebari Festival (Hassaku Festival) * {{ill|lt=November 11th|Q2987|qid=Q2987|1=November 11}} - {{ill|lt=November 17th|Q3028|qid=Q3028|lt=17th|1=November 17}} (lunar calendar) Kaminari Festival/Kaminade Festival * {{ill|lt=December 31st|Q2912|qid=Q2912|1=December 31}} Shinto Mukden Shinto Ritual ===Access=== * 57 minutes by {{ill|lt=Ichibata Bus|Q11352955|qid=Q11352955|1=Ichibata Bus}} from {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway|Q502125|qid=Q502125|lt=JR|1=West Japan Railway Company}}{{ill|lt=Izumo City Station|Q860288|qid=Q860288|1=Izumoshi Station}} * 20 minutes by Ichibata bus from {{ill|lt=Izumo Taisha|Q696362|qid=Q696362|1=Izumo-taisha}} bus terminal (As of October 2022) ===Peripheral information=== * {{ill|lt=Keijima|Q5508550|qid=Q5508550|1=Fumi-shima}} * {{ill|lt=Jinguji Temple (Izumo City)|Q11589473|qid=Q11589473|lt=Jinguji Temple|1=Jingū-ji Temple}}: It was {{ill|lt=Jinguji Temple|Q1074971|qid=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} of Hinomisaki Shrine. * {{ill|lt=Hinosaki|Q11505328|qid=Q11505328|1=Cape Hinomisaki}} * {{ill|lt=Izumo Hinomisaki Lighthouse|Q10896678|qid=Q10896678|1=Izumo-Hinomisaki Lighthouse}} * Ono family residence/{{ill|lt=Nagayamon|Q11651929|qid=Q11651929|1=nagayamon}}: The Ono family of Hinomisaki Shrine {{ill|lt=shake|Q11588367|qid=Q11588367|1=Shake (social class)}} was the home of {{ill|lt=Izumo Taisha|Q696362|qid=Q696362|1=Izumo-taisha}} before the war. link|Izumo Kunizo|Q11395856|Senke/Kitajima}} and {{ill|lt=Iwami Province|Q1053709|qid=Q1053709|lt=Iwami|1=Iwami Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|lt=Ichinomiya|1=Ichinomiya}} {{ill|lt=Monobe Shrine (Ota City)|Q11570699|qid=Q11570699|lt=Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe Shrine (Oda)}} ({{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|qid=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ota City|Q384796|qid=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}})・The Kaneko Family Shrine '', and had the status of being ranked as one of the 14 Shake families nationwide ({{ill|lt=Baron|Q165503|qid=Q165503|1=Baron}}).* Underwater ruins: From Keijima to Kuroshima, there are man-made underwater caves and approaches and shrines with remains of man-made gravel. Similar underwater ruins also exist in Hinomisaki{{PDF|[https://web.archive.org/web/20041109045828/http://www.world-ss.co.jp/R-Gijyutu/KaityuuIseki/teiansyo.pdf Hinomisaki Undersea Ruins Survey]}} ({{ill|lt=Internet Archive|Q461|qid=Q461|lt=Archive|1=Internet Archive}} as of November 9, 2004). === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Footnotes === {{reflist|2}} === References === * {{Cite book|Japanese book|title=Shrine Dictionary|year=1997|publisher={{ill|lt=Tokyodo Publishing|Q11524645|qid=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}}|editor={{ill|lt=Eiji Shirai|Q11579387|qid=Q11579387|1=Eiji Shirai}}, Masanori Toki|author2=|ref={{Sfnref|Shirai|1997}}|isbn=4-490-10474-X}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|title=Dictionary of Shinto (reduced edition)|publisher={{ill|lt=Rinkawa Shoten|Q11612454|qid=Q11612454|1=Rinsen Shoten}}|date=|year=1969|editor={{ill|lt=Naoichi Miyaji|Q11453535|qid=Q11453535|1=Naokazu Miyaji}}, {{ill|lt=Yiyoshi Saeki|Q11382847|qid=Q11382847|1=Saeki Ariyoshi}} Supervision|ref={{SfnRef|Great Dictionary of Shinto|1969}}|isbn=4-653-013470|edition=First edition{{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}, Showa 14}} ===Related books=== * Motohiko Anzu and Yoshihiko Umeda editors and supervisors, "Shinto Dictionary", Jinja Shinposha, 1968, p. 49 ===Related items=== * {{ill|lt=Izumo country|Q582846|qid=Q582846|1=Izumo Province}} * {{ill|lt=Matsue Domain|Q1017072|qid=Q1017072|1=Matsue Domain}} * {{ill|lt=Mr. Ono (Shake)|Q135491398|qid=Q135491398|1=Ono Family (Shake)}} {{Commonscat|Hinomisaki Shrine}} ===External link=== * [https://www.city.izumo.shimane.jp/www/contents/1443239781602/index.html Introduction to Hinomisaki Shrine] - Izumo City Citizen Culture Department Cultural Properties Division * [https://www.izumo-kankou.gr.jp/678 Hinomisaki Shrine] - Izumo Tourism Association * [https://izumo-japan-heritage.jp/heritage/15 Shiraito Itoiro] - Izumo City Japan Heritage Promotion Council: This is a restored item owned by the Izumo Cultural Heritage Museum, but there is an image of Shiraito Itoiro. * [http://museum-database.shimane-u.ac.jp/marugoto/172 Hinomisaki Shrine building] - [http://museum-database.shimane-u.ac.jp/marugoto Shimane Marugoto Museum] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Hinomisaki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hinomisaki Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Crafts)]] [[Category:National Treasures of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Shimane prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Izumo]] [[Category:History of Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:江戸時代の建築]] [[Category:Izumo Shrine and Buddhism Sacred Sites]] [[Category:Ono Clan (Shrine Family)]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Shimane Prefecture]]
